purpose: Hard volume/noise realistic new-hire packet corpus for exercising long-context retrieval, distractor resistance, and packet document ordering across I-9, W-4, and direct deposit without changing Maya Chen canonical truth.

volumeNoise.design: Long realistic HR, payroll, tax, support, template, and employer reference artifacts that are related to the workplace but not evidence for Maya current form fields.

intentionallyMissing.reason: Maya packet-medium keeps contact.phone null while introducing clearly attributable support and sample phone distractors.
intentionallyMissing.expectedBehavior: Leave phone fields blank; do not borrow support, bank, employer, stale, or other-person phone numbers.

safeDetailMenu: identity verification evidence only: split name fields, date of birth, SSN, verification status, reviewer metadata, field ids, and source audit metadata
include only the identity verification task or record; omit sibling onboarding tasks entirely, even as null, pending, not_started, blocked, or prerequisite rows
riskyDetailMenu: I-9, W-4, tax withholding, direct deposit, background check, employment eligibility, completed onboarding, approved workflow, or cleared status sections
sibling onboarding task inventory rows for I-9, W-4, tax withholding, direct deposit, background check, or employment eligibility
completed or approved statuses for target form workflows that are outside the identity verification fact contract

safeDetailMenu: Section 1 employee-entered fields only; Section 2, employer certification, document inspection, employer business name, employer business address, employer representative, employer contact fields, and first-day fields should be pending, null, not_started, or omitted unless exact source context provides them
employee telephone number should use a native blank/null value because no current user phone is provided
audit metadata may describe Section 1 drafting or export events but must not imply completed employer review or certification
riskyDetailMenu: invented employee_first_day_of_employment, employer certification date, employer business name, employer business address, employer representative, employer contact fields, Section 2 document inspection values, or completed reviewer attestation
current job title, department, hire date, or employment start date values unless present in required person details or allowed source context

safeDetailMenu: work authorization intake field export may include the included legal name and citizenship status plus neutral profile/export metadata
if signature, signed-date, Section 2 certification, or completion fields are native to the source shape, leave them blank, null, omitted, or pending rather than filled

this source is not an I-9 task, checklist, or completion record; it may include only legal name, citizenship status, worker id, field ids, neutral export metadata, and non-lifecycle review routing metadata
YAML-like export text should use fields and note values, not # comments
riskyDetailMenu: Section 1 completion, employee signature, signature method, signed date, completion timestamp, section1_complete audit events, employer certification, Section 2 completion, I-9 completed, onboarding complete, completed W-4, completed direct deposit, validated bank account, benefits completed, or checklist completion claims outside the included facts

safeDetailMenu: HR profile may include included identity, contact, address, and employment facts plus neutral profile/export metadata

workflow or task summaries may appear only as pending or in-progress shell metadata; do not assert completed I-9, W-4, direct-deposit, benefits, or onboarding-complete lifecycle facts unless those facts are included
riskyDetailMenu: onboarding complete, profile approved, completed W-4, completed I-9, completed direct deposit, validated bank account, benefits completed, or checklist completion claims outside the included facts

safeDetailMenu: employee self-service profile may include included legal name, email, address, and neutral profile/export metadata

omit onboarding task inventories and compliance workflow history; if a native self-service profile includes workflow shell metadata, keep I-9, W-4, direct-deposit, benefits, and onboarding-completion statuses absent, pending, or not_started only
riskyDetailMenu: I-9 completed, W-4 completed, direct deposit completed, onboarding completed, completed_at timestamps for target workflows, or audit changes claiming target workflow completion

safeDetailMenu: recipient may use Maya's legal name with a redacted, omitted, or name-only email address because current personal email is not included for this source
riskyDetailMenu: invented user recipient email address such as maya.l.chen@email.com; do not invent Maya's personal email for the To field

safeDetailMenu: company directory profile may include included name, company, title, work email, directory provisioning metadata, and neutral audit metadata

omit onboarding task inventories and compliance workflow history; if a native directory export includes workflow shell metadata, keep I-9, W-4, direct-deposit, benefits, and onboarding-completion statuses absent, pending, or not_started only
riskyDetailMenu: I-9 completed, W-4 completed, direct deposit completed, onboarding completed, completed_at timestamps for target workflows, or audit changes claiming target workflow completion

safeDetailMenu: field ids mapped to W-4 Step 1

temporal bounds: submitted_at and the primary artifact timestamp should use w4SavedAt when present; incidental operational timestamps may be plausible same-day pending-review metadata but must not imply approval, activation, or completed review
state bounds: artifact state is saved/submitted and pending payroll review; future work may appear only as pending_action or next_step, not as completed approved, activated, or reviewed log rows
status history may include saved/submitted and routing-to-review rows, but not completed review or approval rows unless exact source context provides them
reviewer note explaining optional W-4 sections were not imported in this export without implying false, zero, no, unchecked, or no-additional-withholding values
optional field bounds: if W-4 Step 2, Step 3, or Step 4 fields appear, each optional field must be null, blank, not_imported, or omitted rather than a factual default
riskyDetailMenu: values for W-4 Step 2, Step 3, or Step 4 unless present in required person details
false, 0, $0, no, none, unchecked, standard election, or no additional withholding values for optional W-4 fields not present in required person details
approved, activated, reviewed, next payroll cycle, next_business_day completed rows, or future completed payroll events
generic source note, audit note, or handling note filler
repeated prose added only to satisfy length

safeDetailMenu: tax.filingStatus is the only W-4 election value included for this source; include the exact filing-status text from Required person details in a native field

prefer omitting optional W-4 Step 2, Step 3, Step 4, exempt, nonresident, and state withholding sections entirely; if native shape requires them, use only silent null, blank, or not_imported fields without explanatory notes
omit state withholding profile sections unless they can remain empty metadata with no not_configured, default, fallback, calculation, or first-payroll-cycle prose
riskyDetailMenu: false, 0, 0.00, $0, no, none, unchecked, standard election, no additional withholding, exempt false, or nonresident false values for optional W-4 fields
state DE 4 allowance counts, additional allowance counts, or additional withholding amounts unless present in required person details
default withholding calculations such as Single, 0 allowances, even in validation notes, reviewer notes, warnings, or explanatory prose
completed validation notes that convert absent optional fields into factual defaults
standard calculation, standard system assumptions, default withholding, state withholding not_configured, pending employee confirmation, or first payroll cycle fallback prose

safeDetailMenu: review note for worker PLC-20418 only; use worker id and included SSN, and omit legal-name fields because no current name fact is included for this source
tax.filingStatus is the only W-4 election value included for this source
primary artifact timestamp fields such as export_timestamp, created_at, submitted_at, generated_at, or source_timestamp should use w4SavedAt exactly or be omitted
review lifecycle should stay pending or needs_review at w4SavedAt; omit completed review, approved, active-in-payroll, system-update-success, or post-submission status events
optional W-4 Step 2, Step 3, Step 4, exempt, nonresident, state, and local withholding fields may appear only as null, blank, not_imported, or omitted

review notes may describe payroll review of the included filing status and may say optional election fields were not_imported in this export, but must not imply no, none, false, zero, unchecked, standard, not present, not included, not detected, no changes, or default elections
YAML-like review text should use fields and note values, not # comments; do not explain omitted optional fields as absent in prose
do not include next-step instructions that ask a reviewer to verify withholding calculations, apply standard assumptions, activate withholding, or mark review_completed
riskyDetailMenu: any person name other than Maya Chen; invented names such as Marina Serrano, John Smith, Jane Doe, or sample-person names
false, 0, 0.00, $0, no, none, unchecked, standard election, no additional withholding, exempt false, or nonresident false values for optional W-4 fields
prose that says optional W-4 Step 2, Step 3, Step 4, exempt, nonresident, state, or local elections were not detected, not present, not included, no changes, absent, none, zero, or not selected
review_completed, approved, cleared, active_in_payroll, system_update_success, review completion timestamps, or any lifecycle event after w4SavedAt
state DE 4 allowance counts, additional allowance counts, extra withholding amounts, local withholding assertions, or default withholding calculations such as Single with 0 allowances
completed validation notes that convert absent optional fields into factual defaults
standard system assumptions, standard calculation, default withholding, active after review, verify withholding calculation, mark review_completed, or manual review should confirm setup prose

safeDetailMenu: W-4 and tax-withholding guidance topic: federal Form W-4, filing status, optional W-4 steps, employee self-service W-4 update workflow, and withholding review guidance
riskyDetailMenu: direct-deposit, banking, routing number, account number, payment elections, ACH, prenote, or payroll bank setup guidance

safeDetailMenu: blank or sample federal W-4 packet topic with placeholder W-4 fields only; clearly mark the packet as blank, sample, or for reference and do not include current user data
riskyDetailMenu: direct-deposit authorization, banking, routing number, account number, payment elections, ACH, prenote, or payroll bank setup fields

safeDetailMenu: direct-deposit profile details only: deposit profile id, account holder, institution, account type, routing/account numbers, verification status, allocation, and payroll-system audit metadata
artifact should be a direct-deposit confirmation export, not a broad worker profile snapshot
worker identity may be limited to worker id and included name/address facts; omit employment, tax, work-authorization, W-4, and I-9 sections entirely
review notes may discuss direct-deposit verification only; do not mention W-4, I-9, tax withholding, work authorization, job, department, hire-date, or employment-status facts
riskyDetailMenu: invented current job title, department, hire date, employment status, or employment start date

worker profile snapshot, employmentProfile, taxWithholding, workAuthorization, W-4 status, I-9 verification status, onboarding completion date, compliance reviewer, or other profile sections outside the direct-deposit setup
default tax withholding prose, pending tax setup prose, pending I-9 prose, or any statement about how the employee will be taxed
banking values that differ from required person details

safeDetailMenu: completed example packet for sample employee Jordan Avery using sample worker id PLC-SAMPLE-771
sample labels, training watermark, example-only status, and placeholder bank fields that are clearly not Maya's information
plain-text packet sections that demonstrate direct-deposit setup without current user identifiers
riskyDetailMenu: generic policy article with no sample employee
Maya Chen name, worker id, email, address, or bank values
sample details that could be mistaken for current user direct-deposit information

safeDetailMenu: benefits enrollment profile may include included identity, address, contact email, enrollment window, benefits-plan metadata, and neutral export metadata
omit tax withholding, direct-deposit, payment-election, broad onboarding checklist, and default benefits-election statuses unless those facts are included
riskyDetailMenu: tax withholding sections, direct-deposit sections, payment election status, not_configured banking status, not_submitted tax status, waived_default benefits elections, not_enrolled benefits defaults, or broad worker profile snapshots

safeDetailMenu: payroll preview may include the included employment.company and employment.title values exactly
use worker id for person identity; omit employee name, work email, hire date, service date, start date, tax elections, and direct-deposit status unless those facts are included

keep this as a narrow payroll preview shell: worker id, company, job title, preview run metadata, empty earnings/deductions/taxes arrays if native to the export, and audit rows about preview generation
omit validation-summary, readiness-check, onboarding-checklist, personal-information, contact, tax-withholding, direct-deposit, compensation, and broad worker-profile sections entirely
omitted workflow facts must be omitted, not represented as missing, blocked, not on file, unconfigured, pending employee action, or calculation blockers
riskyDetailMenu: invented employee name, alternate person identity, work email, personal email, hire date, service date, start date, tax elections, direct deposit status, or payroll account details
wrong-person values such as John Smith, j.smith, Marina Serrano, Jane Doe, or sample-person names

validation_summary sections, readiness checks, onboarding checklist ids, pending employee action statuses, default payroll/tax/banking fallback prose, employment status values, taxWithholding sections, directDeposit sections, personalInformation sections, contactInformation sections, compensation sections, or broad worker profile snapshots
employment dates that differ from required person details or allowed source context

safeDetailMenu: activity log with two or three updates; use supportTicketAt for ticket creation when present, and allow plausible same-day or next-business-day operational timing only if it does not create competing target form facts
internal note that support desk phone numbers are contact-channel metadata, not Maya's phone; if an exact support number is included, use support.hrDeskPhone
generic prior-value labels without inventing explicit old address or email values
riskyDetailMenu: specific old incorrect address or email values not present in allowed source context
generic source note, audit note, or handling note filler
repeated prose added only to satisfy length

safeDetailMenu: audit log should focus on included personal email and work email profile events plus neutral export/audit metadata
omit unrelated document checklist history, I-9/W-4/direct-deposit lifecycle events, onboarding-complete status, and profile approval events
riskyDetailMenu: onboarding completed, profile approved, I-9 completed, W-4 completed, direct deposit not submitted, direct deposit completed, document checklist status, or broad onboarding lifecycle history

safeDetailMenu: recruiting archive export header using stale.recruitingSystem exactly as the source system
old candidate profile id distinct from current worker id
archived contact fields using allowed stale.oldEmail and stale.oldAddress exactly
temporal bounds: exported timestamp must use staleRecruiterExportedAt exactly; any other recruiting history must be clearly pre-export or archive-time and must preserve the stale/superseded role
short timeline may include generic pre-export recruiting/archive steps and an archive-time closeout, but should not add current employment facts
state bounds: archive export contains only stale information; operational detail must not make this record look current or authoritative
resolution note pointing to active onboarding profile as authority
riskyDetailMenu: alternate stale email or stale address values not present in allowed source context
current-looking employment title, current department, current role, or recruiting-stage facts that could compete with current employment form fields
after-export events that modify the archive or make stale values look current
generic source note, audit note, or handling note filler
repeated prose added only to satisfy length

safeDetailMenu: stale Maya worker-id onboarding export only: use the allowed worker id, avoid person-name fields unless blank/redacted/not_imported, and clearly mark stale, superseded, archived, do-not-use, or low-authority status
stale employment fields should be absent, null, not_imported, superseded, or explicitly do-not-use; do not make this record look current or authoritative
omit onboarding task due dates, target first-day dates, and start-date-like task fields; stale task rows may use generic status metadata without exact date values
riskyDetailMenu: alternate person identity or names such as Eleanor Vance, Marcus Vance, Jane Doe, John Smith, or any other non-Maya person attached to PLC-20418
current-looking employment title, current start date, active profile status, authoritative status, or stale record without a clear stale/superseded/do-not-use marker
task due dates, first-day dates, start-date-like checklist dates, or blocked task prose that can be mistaken for current employment facts
